{"key_event":"War of the Pacific and nitrate annexation","reasoning":"War mobilization, northern nitrate revenues, frontier incorporation in Araucanía, and secular civil registries moved Chile from a strong toward a very strong state, though not one with total social penetration. Civil society gained somewhat through parties, Congress, press, urban associations, and early labor organization, but these gains were offset by wartime centralization and the weakening of Mapuche autonomy and Church institutional power.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":6.3,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":8.6}
